titel: Minutes from the monetary policy meeting held on May 6, 2026.
html:

Published: 2026-05-13

At the monetary policy meeting held on May 6, 2026, the board decided to leave the key interest rate unchanged at 1.75 percent.

The minutes of the meeting provide insight into the discussions that led to the decision and show how the different board members argued their positions.

The board typically holds eight monetary policy meetings per year to make decisions about monetary policy. Approximately five business days after each monetary policy meeting, the minutes are published.
tldr: The interest rate will remain at 1.75 percent. This means that the cost of borrowing and the return on savings will not change for the time being.
lattlast:
At a meeting held on May 6, 2026, the bank's management decided to maintain the key interest rate at 1.75 percent. The key interest rate is the rate that influences the cost of borrowing money and the amount of interest earned when saving at the bank.

This decision means that there will be no changes for households or businesses at this time. The meeting minutes provide more details about the discussions that led to this decision, and outline the views of the various members of the management team.

The management typically holds eight such meetings each year to determine the direction of monetary policy. After each meeting, a record is published, which is a written summary of what was decided and discussed.
